reasoning_model_2: Model 2 successfully generates the requested paralinguistic feature ('打嗝') with high fidelity. model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 2 accurately interprets '打嗝' (hiccup) and integrates distinct, realistic hiccup sounds throughout the speech, affecting the rhythm naturally. Model 1 merely inserts a throat-clearing sound (which sounds like a cough) at the beginning and speaks the rest of the sentence normally, failing to convey the specific 'hiccup' characteristic. Furthermore, Model 1 has more significant text deviations (missing a middle filler and adding a final particle) compared to Model 2's omission of an initial filler. Model 2 demonstrates superior control over the specific non-verbal vocalization requested.
